title: Gate Maintenance, Upgrades, Metrics, and Capstone · Quality Engineering Board
summary: Fresh repository setup, configuration review, failing-gate diagnosis, sanitizer, CI security, and maintenance cases across TypeScript, Python, and native code. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.
Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.
Keep one canonical config per concern and document generated files.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run
steps.instruction: Order the Repo Purpose evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Repo Purpose assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates maintenance. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Repo Purpose, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a reusable quality-gated repository template.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every new repository should use the same maximum gate set. This fails the Repo Purpose contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Clang AddressSanitizer and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (Clang 23 development documentation) — Controls native-code runtime sanitizer purpose, build profiles, output, limitations, and security cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.

hints: Name the Repo Purpose precondition or invariant that the exam case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed exam case derived from this module: gates maintenance. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Every new repository should use the same maximum gate set.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Build the performance evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-capstone-defense-jr339u: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Capstone Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-capstone-defense-jr339u:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

steps.title: Submit the certification defense
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Capstone Defense applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend each gate by risk, ownership, feedback time, evidence, and maintenance plan.
